basics.name: Jack Burnett
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: PhD researcher in Interactive AI at the University of Bristol, developing democratised pipelines for accessible gaming controller design through parametric modelling and N-of-1 co-design. Qualified teacher with experience across secondary, further, and higher education.

education.institution: University of the West of England
University of the West of England

projects.name: Co-Designing Controllers: A Pipeline for Customisation of Gaming Input Devices using Generative AI and Parametric Design
projects.summary: PhD Thesis focusing on the impact and need for democratised controller development, which enables this design space through the use of parametric design and N-of-1 co-design studies.

work.name: Department for Education
work.summary: Supporting within the Behavioural Insights Unit with designing and delivering co-creation workshops, alongside conducting literature reviews and data analysis to inform policy design. Contributed to AI Strategy development work.

work.name: University of Bristol
work.summary: Assisting the teaching and lecturing within BSc modules such as 'Interactive Devices' and 'Interaction and Society'. Assisted with the supervision of BSc dissertations.

work.name: University of Bristol
work.summary: Planning and delivering seminars, workshops and other academic sessions to young people aged 11-18 to promote university engagement.

work.name: Forward Futures Ltd.
work.summary: Teaching network security, both for in-house systems and cloud technologies; qualifications taught were part of CISCO and Microsoft qualification suites.

work.name: Weston College
work.summary: Teaching across BTEC, T-Level, and BSc courses within computing. Primarily taught units within data analysis and software engineering.

work.name: Cabot Learning Federation
work.summary: Teaching computer science and DT for years 7-9, alongside A-Level computer science and BTEC Esports for years 12-13. Tutor for a year 7 group.

work.name: Caldicot Comprehensive School
work.summary: Teaching computer science for years 7-10, alongside A-Level computer science for year 12.

work.name: Kingsmead School Wiveliscombe
work.summary: Teaching computer science for years 7-10, alongside BTEC iMedia science for year 10.

awards.title: Device Prototyping And Production Summer School Award
awards.summary: Paper selected for a £1,000 award for demonstrated potential for impact, from a pool of submissions to the summer school. Funding directed toward further development of device prototypes.

volunteering.summary: Ambassador for Cats Protection, raising awareness of the charity, enhancing children’s understanding of feline welfare, and providing information sessions for local groups.
Assisting in the strategic management and oversight of the academy and ensuring that it meets its legal and ethical responsibilities for supporting students with SEND.
Printing and assembling controller modifications for UK-based gamers with physical disabilities, directly supporting the accessibility research focus of ongoing PhD work.

certificates.name: School Health for Children and Adolescents
